behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (HTTP/raw-body parsing -> signature verifier -> queue). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to trigger mismatch logic. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked request object can hide raw-body handling bugs, so Unit is too low. Rejected System: behavior text only requires reject/forward logic, not real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler with the real web framework request parsing path and real signature-check module, send a mismatched signature, assert HTTP 401 and assert queue enqueue method is not called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is ordering across modules (handler response path vs persistent queue write). Unit cannot prove cross-module ordering. REAL-INFRA: no real external network/subprocess required if queue is local persistent component. Rejected System: behavior text specifies ordering with persistent queue write, not OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Instrument the persistent queue write and response send points in an in-process app; assert the queue write completes before 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires wiring handler, queue handoff, and downstream worker boundary. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced without real OS/network by making downstream processing intentionally slow in-process. Rejected System: behavior text is about not awaiting downstream completion, not real network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use a downstream processor that blocks on a controllable promise; verify HTTP response is sent before that promise resolves.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is a wall-clock latency/concurrency guarantee under load; event-loop scheduling and actual HTTP serving behavior matter. MOCK-HIDE: fake timers/in-process stubs can miss contention and real timeout behavior. Rejected Integration: behavior explicitly requires 'within 3 seconds wall-clock' under concurrency, which is a real runtime boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service process, create concurrent slow downstream load from earlier events, send a new webhook over real HTTP, and assert end-to-end response time < 3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: duplicate prevention depends on state handoff across requests and queue persistence/dedup path. Unit on a pure function misses real state interaction. REAL-INFRA: local persistent store/module wiring is sufficient; no external OS/network prerequisite. Rejected System: behavior text does not require distributed/networked dedup semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same event ID twice through the handler against the real dedup/persistence path; assert only one queue write record exists.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is mapping queue backpressure signal to HTTP status/header across module boundary. Unit may miss translation wiring. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; queue can deterministically return 'full'. Rejected System: real capacity enforcement is covered by behavior 7; this behavior only checks handler response mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue module (or test double at module boundary) to return a 'full' condition; assert handler responds 503 and includes Retry-After.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires real queue persistence/capacity logic, not a mocked queue. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue is still Integration per level defs. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on queue's own capacity rule, with no required subprocess/real external network dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with a small capacity, enqueue until full, then enqueue one more and assert a capacity rejection is returned and persisted state does not exceed limit.
